title: mentha × schultziana var. carinthiaca
intro: Mentha × schultziana var. carinthiaca, also known as the Corn Mint, is a variety of hybrid mint plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The Corn Mint has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful in treating digestive issues and relieving headaches and nausea. The plant's health benefits are attributed to its ability to ease digestive problems, soothe respiratory issues, and reduce inflammation, making it a popular choice for herbal remedies. Corn Mint is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and salves, and can be found in various forms including fresh leaves, dried herbs, and essential oil.
